era_context: Egypt's Fourth Dynasty under Khafre; pyramid construction at Giza. The Akkadian Empire under Sargon is consolidating power in Mesopotamia. This period represents the height of Old Kingdom Egypt.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Mesopotamia (limited specific records for this year)

events.title: Khafre's pyramid construction continues
events.description: Khafre's mortuary complex continues under construction. The pyramid, now approaching completion or in its final phases, represents the culmination of Old Kingdom architectural and engineering achievement. The associated temples, causeway, and valley temple were being constructed during this period. These structures served as the focus of the pharaoh's mortuary cult and reinforced the centralised authority of the Egyptian state.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Khafre's pyramid complex

events.certainty_note: The pyramid is archaeologically confirmed; the specific year is assigned by convention.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is essentially undocumented outside of Egyptian monumental archaeology. The surviving record is heavily biased toward elite and royal activities.
